title: Suspected University-Wide Ransomware
subtitle: Live incident response, decentralized research university, single flat Active Directory domain (~1,600 servers, ~7,300 workstations). No central SIEM or 24/7 SOC; holiday run-up, thin coverage. Exercise run.

severity.text: Impact is already materialized (files are encrypted now), so this is not a "possible" risk. Two correlated signals in the same window on a flat, unsegmented ~8,900-host estate, plus loss of the normal coordination channel (email) and thin holiday staffing, all amplify it. Re-rate at every material change.

history.reason: encryption confirmed across shares, correlated with mail outage on a flat domain

exec: Three simultaneous signals are being treated as one active intrusion until proven otherwise: the mail platform is down and silent, a phishing report (link + Excel) sits on record from earlier, and files are being found encrypted across shares.

The most likely story, unconfirmed, is a phished foothold, easy lateral movement across a single flat Active Directory domain, escalation to privileged access, then mail knocked out and ransomware pushed broadly. Scope, whether the attacker still has access, whether backups are clean, and whether data was stolen are all still unknown.
priorities: Stand up out-of-band command and comms; email and AD cannot be trusted.
Contain by network isolation, not power-off: cut internet egress, the backup plane, the virtualization management plane, and privileged authentication first and quietly.
Preserve domain-controller and volatile evidence before anyone re-images.
Pin the awareness timestamp and open the GDPR breach register now.
Call the cyber-insurer before engaging any outside firm, then DFIR and the sector CSIRT (SURFcert).
askOfManagement: Authorize the out-of-band war room, the four quiet cuts, and the insurer call now; hold the pay/no-pay and rebuild-vs-restore decisions for a deliberated council/board verdict.

inherent.rationale: Encryption is confirmed and spreading potential is high on a flat domain with no segmentation and no central monitoring; the adverse impact is already observed, so likelihood is Almost certain, not Possible. Data-theft and backup-loss tails are open.

residual.rationale: Disciplined isolation, evidence preservation, and gated recovery reduce further spread and protect the ability to rebuild cleanly. Residual stays high because probable prior exfiltration, possible backup compromise, and attacker persistence surviving recovery cannot yet be excluded.

notifications.label: GDPR Art. 33 breach notification
notifications.trigger: Personal-data breach where risk cannot be excluded; file a phased notification even if scope is incomplete.
notifications.status_reason: Risk to data subjects cannot be excluded on the facts known; precautionary phased filing.

notifications.label: GDPR Art. 33(5) internal register
notifications.trigger: All breaches must be documented internally, regardless of notifiability.

notifications.label: GDPR Art. 34 notification to individuals
notifications.trigger: Required only on a high-risk determination, which needs scope not yet established.
notifications.status_reason: Cannot determine high-risk until scope and data categories are established; breadth and channel routed to the controller.

notifications.label: Executive Board briefing
notifications.trigger: Live suspected ransomware incident with personal-data exposure.

notifications.label: Sector CSIRT engagement
notifications.trigger: Education/research sector CSIRT accepts voluntary reports now; standard first call.

notifications.label: Cyber-insurer notification
notifications.trigger: Engaging DFIR or a negotiator before insurer notice can void cover.
notifications.status_reason: Call first, before any outside firm; read the actual policy wording today.

notifications.label: NIS2 / Cyberbeveiligingswet (Cbw) notification (24h/72h/1-month)
notifications.status_reason: Cbw enters into force 15 Aug 2026; NOT in force on 2026-07-21. Re-check after that date and confirm entity scope.

notifications.label: DORA incident reporting
notifications.status_reason: DORA applies to financial entities and their ICT providers; a research university is out of scope. Not triggered.

notifications.label: CRA (Cyber Resilience Act) reporting
notifications.status_reason: CRA reporting duties fall on manufacturers of products with digital elements, not on a university operating an estate. Not triggered.

notifications.label: Current-law Wbni (AED designation duty)
notifications.status_reason: No blanket university designation found; check this institution's own designation file. Not confirmed triggered today.

notifications.label: Law-enforcement report (politie aangifte)
notifications.status_reason: No statutory deadline; recommended and timed with DFIR/insurer after evidence is secured, not an immediate legal gate.

awareness.basis: Designated incident owner confirmed, on signals 1+3 together, a suspected ransomware event touching personal-data systems; not the first helpdesk ticket, not gated on knowing which data.

data_categories: Staff and student identity and contact data
Possibly special-category data (health, research-subject data) - unconfirmed
breachRegister.approx_data_subjects: Unknown at filing; potentially tens of thousands (staff + students) - to be scoped
breachRegister.approx_records: Unknown at filing; file shares across ~1,600 servers in scope
breachRegister.likely_consequences: Loss of availability (encryption) confirmed; loss of confidentiality (exfiltration) cannot be excluded. Potential identity fraud, distress, and disruption to studies/employment if data was stolen.
measures_taken: Network isolation of egress, backup plane, hypervisor management, and Tier-0 authentication
Estate-wide hold order to preserve volatile evidence
Out-of-band command and comms stood up
DFIR and sector CSIRT engagement initiated

risk_assessment.art33_risk: Risk to rights and freedoms cannot be excluded - notify (phased).
risk_assessment.rationale: Availability impact is certain; a confidentiality impact is plausible on a flat domain reached to Domain-Admin level. Absent an egress baseline, exfiltration may never be excludable, so the posture is precautionary.

timeline: Phishing email reported to service desk (link + Excel). Action taken unknown.
Mail server unreachable and silent; email down (send + receive).
Encrypted files / altered extensions / possible ransom note reported across shares.
Declared SEV-1; roles named; decision log opened.
Email and AD-federated tools declared untrusted; out-of-band channel + physical war room stood up.
Hold order issued: no touch, no reboot, no re-image on affected hosts.
Perimeter egress locked to allowlist (cut C2 cheaply, keep power on).
Backup plane isolated; backup jobs paused; immutable copies checked.
verify immutable/offline backups exist: backup lead
Hypervisor management plane isolated; Tier-0 accounts frozen.
verify virtualization platform exists: infra lead
Working hypothesis set: ONE intrusion, phishing report as unconfirmed candidate entry.
Awareness timestamp pinned; GDPR breach register opened.
Pay/no-pay and rebuild-vs-restore referred to the council/board.
First board brief; insurer policy pulled; DFIR + SURFcert engaged.

roles: Holds tempo and final call authority.
One job: timestamp every decision with a one-line reason.
Senior sysadmin; authority to veto evidence-destroying moves, even the commander's.
Looped in from minute one given likely personal-data exposure.
AD/Windows, network, backup/storage, plus a faculty-IT liaison per affected faculty.
triage.hypothesis: One intrusion. Same window, same estate, and a flat single domain is exactly the topology where one stolen credential explains both a dead mail server and encrypted shares elsewhere. The phishing report is an open thread to test, not a proven cause. Forensics owns disproving the single-incident theory; until then this is one case file, not three tickets.

cutFirst: 1. Internet egress (server VLANs to tight allowlist)
Kills command-and-control cheaply, low evidence cost, no one inside notices immediately.
Isolate repositories, pause jobs so encrypted data cannot overwrite good backups, rotate backup-console credentials, confirm immutable/offline copies untouched.
A hypervisor compromise is a mass-encryption multiplier.
Freeze Domain Admin / Tier-0 use; do not push AD-wide changes from an AD we do not yet trust.
dial.widen: Quarantine VLANs for subnets showing new encryption; block SMB/RDP lateral paths at the firewall; suspend non-essential VPN. Re-decide the dial every 30 to 60 minutes.

carveOuts.treatment: Network-isolate at most, with continuous physical-safety monitoring
Assess each with the facility owner; never cold-cut
Isolate only with continuous monitoring; preserve live experiments

dial.pctNote: Targets by T0+90. Host-level isolation across ~8,900 assets is the long pole; expect low single-digit percent given the manual, faculty-by-faculty pace. Reported as-is, not rounded up.

evidence.rule: Network-isolate immediately, but do not power off and do not re-image a host until at least a memory capture and a process/network snapshot exist, or the commander explicitly accepts and logs the evidence loss. Isolation stops spread as well as power-off does, without destroying memory-resident evidence.
tiers: Live memory + process/network state on the mail server, first few encrypted servers, any DC showing interactive attacker logon, and the phishing-reported workstation if identifiable.

Ransom note text/location from several hosts; a sample encrypted file plus a known-good original; DC state (NTDS.dit, SYSTEM hive, Security/System logs) BEFORE any password reset or ticket invalidation; mail server image or at least transport/message-tracking logs before any reboot.
EDR/AV console exports, firewall/proxy/DNS egress logs, VPN/remote-access logs, backup-server job history (check for attacker deletion), any netflow.
evidence.custody: With email down: paper or offline USB-carried log; per item record collector, UTC+local time, SHA-256 at collection and re-verified at each handoff, and storage location. Evidence goes to write-once media or an isolated evidence store with no domain trust, not a share on the possibly-compromised domain.
evidence.scope: Patient zero, dwell time, and blast radius are NOT yet established. With no SIEM, the timeline is a manual correlation of scattered, faculty-owned sources (DC auth logs first, then the phished endpoint, EDR/AV history, file-share modification times, VPN and backup logs). In a flat domain, "how far did it spread" largely equals "where did the stolen credentials work". External DFIR handoff for the deep timeline is expected.
evidence.exfil: Cannot be excluded. "No evidence of theft" and "evidence of no theft" are different claims; we have neither. This environment likely lacks an egress baseline, so exclusion may never be achievable, not merely "not yet". Legal and comms must plan on that basis.
evidence.topRisk: The biggest evidence risk right now is not the attacker, it is us: stressed faculty IT re-imaging machines to "just fix it" before capture. The hold order must reach every faculty, not just central IT.

register.custodyNote: Captured before any reboot; chain-of-custody sheet CoC-001

register.item: DC state (NTDS.dit, SYSTEM hive, Security/System logs)
register.custodyNote: Captured BEFORE any password reset or ticket invalidation; CoC-002
Pair kept for decryptor/identification work; verify hash at DFIR handoff

decisions.rationale: materialized encryption + correlated mail outage on a flat domain
command clarity before any technical action

decisions.decision: Email/AD declared untrusted; out-of-band channel + war room
decisions.rationale: cannot coordinate over a channel the attacker may hold
cut C2 cheaply, power stays on
parsimony on a flat domain; forensics to disprove

decisions.decision: Insurer policy pulled before DFIR engagement

decisions.rationale: bet-the-organization calls the response team does not settle alone

eradication.approach: hybrid (rebuild Tier-0 greenfield, restore data from verified-clean media)
sequence: Complete scoping: patient zero, dwell, blast radius, exfil read.
Identify and remove persistence across reached hosts.
Rotate all credentials with DC state captured first.
Rebuild or restore ONLY from verified-clean, offline/immutable media.
Re-establish AD trust from a known-good baseline.

gates.note: Depends on confirming patient zero; phishing candidate unproven.
Requires forensic scope of reached hosts.
Blocked until DC state captured (EV-002 done); can proceed next.

gates.gate: Backups proven clean AND proven offline/immutable at time of compromise
gates.note: Backup lead verifying from a trusted console.

gates.gate: AD trust re-established from known-good baseline
gates.note: Sequenced after credential rotation and rebuild decision.
Carved out and monitored; no cold cut applied.
Out of scope for this entity.

eradication.cleanroomNote: Rebuild the Tier-0 baseline in an isolated clean-room network with no trust to the compromised domain; validate images against known-good hashes before any reconnection.
reconnectOrder: Rebuilt Tier-0 (DCs, PKI) in clean-room, validated.
Core services (identity, DNS) on the new baseline.
Faculty subnets, one at a time, after host validation.

escalations: Pay vs no-pay, with mandatory sanctions-screening of actor/wallet before any contact (a payment to a sanctioned entity is itself a violation).
Rebuild-greenfield vs restore the domain (a flat compromised domain often argues for a hardened rebuild).
Notification breadth under GDPR Art. 34: whether and how widely to tell individuals, and by what channel with email down.

commsLog.statement: War room location and secure channel invite; email is not to be used.
Live suspected ransomware with possible personal-data exposure; response underway; decisions pending.
We are responding to an IT disruption affecting email and some systems; more information to follow through official channels.

doNotClaim.rule: One designated spokesperson; all regulator/press/law-enforcement contact through Legal. Nothing forensics has not confirmed is stated in public.
forbidden_until_forensic_signoff: "Data was not accessed" or "no data was stolen"
"No health / financial / research data was affected"
Any statement on whether a ransom will be paid
doNotClaim.approved_holding_line: We are responding to an IT disruption affecting email and some systems; we will share verified information through official channels.
doNotClaim.spokesperson: Head of Communications (single point), backed by Legal

assumptions.claim: The estate is virtualized (a hypervisor / vCenter-class management plane exists).
assumptions.basis: Inferred from ~1,600 servers; not stated in the incident inputs.
assumptions.confirm: Infra lead confirms the virtualization platform and its management network; if the servers are mostly physical the "isolate hypervisor plane" step is a no-op and is dropped.

assumptions.claim: Immutable or offline backup copies exist and were untouched at time of compromise.
assumptions.basis: Assumed so that "protect the backup plane" and "restore from clean media" are viable; not stated.
assumptions.confirm: Backup lead verifies immutability/offline status and last-known-good timestamps from a trusted console.

assumptions.claim: The intrusion is one event (mail outage + encryption + prior phishing report linked).
assumptions.basis: Working hypothesis on a flat domain, not yet evidenced.
assumptions.confirm: Forensics correlates DC auth logs, the phishing sample IOCs, and encryption timestamps.

assumptions.claim: Personal and/or special-category data is in scope (drives the GDPR posture).
assumptions.basis: Assumed from the nature of a university; specific data categories unconfirmed.
assumptions.confirm: DPO/forensics identify which data-bearing systems were reached.

assumptions.claim: EDR / endpoint telemetry exists to reconstruct patient zero.
assumptions.basis: Hoped-for, not confirmed; coverage likely inconsistent across autonomous faculties.
assumptions.confirm: Forensics inventories what EDR/AV logging actually exists per faculty.

wentWell: Signals were correlated as one intrusion fast, not triaged as three unrelated tickets.
Network isolation over power-off preserved volatile evidence while stopping spread.
Awareness timestamp was pinned deliberately, so the GDPR clock is defensible.
contributingFactors: Flat single AD domain is the core weakness; no segmentation to slow lateral movement.
No central SIEM or 24/7 monitoring meant the intrusion ran unseen.
No pre-agreed out-of-band comms and no pre-staged forensic tooling/evidence store slowed hour one.
Backup immutability and segmentation of the backup and hypervisor planes are decisive.
Holiday coverage and escalation need a standing on-call plan.

actionItems.item: Introduce AD tiering and segment the server, backup, and hypervisor planes.
Stand up central log collection + MDR with adequate retention.
Pre-stage an out-of-band comms plan and a phone-invited secure channel.
Verify and document immutable/offline backups and last-known-good cadence.
Publish a standing on-call and holiday-coverage escalation plan.

lessons.nextStep: Stand up out-of-band command now (physical war room + a freshly created, phone-invited secure channel) and issue the estate-wide hold order, then execute the four quiet cuts (egress, backup plane, hypervisor management, Tier-0 freeze) in the first 30 minutes. Everything else depends on a trustworthy way to coordinate and a preserved scene.

seats.name: Incident Commander
seats.role: Declares severity and roles, runs triage to contain to recover, keeps the decision log
seats.stance: Network-isolate C2 / backup / hypervisor / privileged auth now, power stays on, hold all restores until eradication and clean-backup verification are proven.
seats.sum: Treats the signals as one active intrusion; contains fast and quietly without powering hosts off; carves out life-safety/OT; escalates pay/no-pay and rebuild-vs-restore rather than settling them.

seats.name: Forensics Lead
seats.role: Preserves evidence and chain of custody, reconstructs the timeline, reads whether exfiltration can be excluded
seats.stance: Preserve DC/identity and volatile evidence first; treat as one incident until disproven; default to exfiltration-not-excluded. Scope and patient zero not yet established.
seats.sum: Low confidence is honest: with no IOC correlation done and a large flat AD with many possible entry points, both the single-incident linkage and phishing-as-patient-zero are still unproven. Biggest near-term risk is well-meaning staff destroying evidence.

seats.name: Legal & Comms (DPO facet)
seats.role: Pins awareness, tracks notification clocks, keeps the breach register, gates external comms and victim-data sharing
seats.stance: File a phased GDPR Art. 33 notification within 72h of awareness regardless of scope; SURFcert now; Cbw not yet live; gate comms and victim-data sharing; route ransom and Art. 34 breadth to the controller.
seats.sum: Verified the live legal picture: GDPR is the only hard statutory clock today; the Dutch NIS2 transposition is not in force until 15 Aug 2026. Insurer must likely be called before any external firm.

seatNote: The three seats do not conflict; they layer. The one tension worth naming is speed: the commander wants to cut fast, forensics wants to capture first. The sequencing rule (isolate at the network layer immediately, capture memory before power-off or re-image, log any accepted evidence loss) resolves it without either side losing.
verified: GDPR Art. 33 = 72h from awareness; phased notification permitted; register all breaches (Art. 33(5)). Source: gdpr-info.eu, EDPB Guidelines 9/2022 v2.0.
AP guidance: file within 72h for ransomware/phishing even if incomplete; holiday/understaffing is not an accepted excuse. Source: autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl.
Cyberbeveiligingswet (Dutch NIS2) enters into force 15 Aug 2026, no transition period; not in force on 2026-07-21. Source: ncsc.nl, Eerste Kamer 36.764.
SURFcert is the education/research sector CSIRT and accepts voluntary reports now. Source: sec.surf.nl.
unverified: The cyber-insurance policy's exact notice and pre-authorization terms (read the policy today).
This university's current Wbni / AED designation status (check the designation file).
The exact wall-clock awareness timestamp (forensics to backfill from tickets/logs).
